title: JTBD — NAICS 493120 Refrigerated Warehousing and Storage

sections.title: Phase 1 — Industry Landscape
sections.content:
### 1.1 CFJ + Output Types + Offering Level

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| CFJ (L7) | Preserve the physical integrity, temperature chain, and legal compliance of perishable goods from inbound receipt through outbound dispatch, with auditable traceability and regulatory conformance |
| Output types | Chilled storage (+2 to +8 °C), Frozen storage (-18 to -25 °C), Deep-freeze / blast (-25 to -40 °C), Pharma-GDP storage (+2 to +8 °C, validated), Cross-dock & VAS |
| Offering level (industry) | L7 ecosystem — service NAICS, no Product BOM |

### 1.2 Segments (cold-storage operator types — by circumstance)

| # | Segment | Defining circumstance |
|---|---|---|
| S1 | Multi-tenant 3PL cold chain | Nordfrost, Frigoscandia, Constellation; multi-customer SLA, mixed temp zones, 24/7 dispatch |
| S2 | Captive producer cold store | Nestlé, Unilever frozen, dairies; single-product, attached to plant, batch-driven |
| S3 | Pharma / GDP-validated cold store | DHL Pharma, World Courier; +2 to +8 °C, full audit trail, IQ/OQ/PQ on every system |
| S4 | Quick-commerce / grocery DC | REWE Frischlogistik, Edeka regional DC, Picnic; high-throughput pick, peak loads at dawn |
| S5 | Blast-freeze / IQF processor | Iglo, Frosta tolling lines; massive intermittent compressor load during freeze cycles |

Filter for Mein Solar's product: **all 5 are addressable** — same MV interface, same revenue stack. S3 (pharma) has the highest availability bar; S1 + S5 have the largest peak-shave economics.

### 1.3 / 1.4 / 1.5 Process VN, BOM, Edges

Reuse VN file (`VN_refrigerated_warehousing_and_storage_sam.md`). No Product BOM (service NAICS). Edges immaterial — BESS sits in horizontal L6H-POWER, not in the food-flow process chain.

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 2.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Anchor unit | Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) |
| Anchor level | **L5** |
| Parent unit | Energy Supply & Electrical Utilities |
| Parent level | L6 (horizontal) |
| Anchor type | PROCESS_VN |
| Parent defines | Job context = "supply reliable, cost-optimized electrical energy to all cold-store loads (refrigeration, MHE, IT/OT, lighting, EV) and protect HACCP temperature chain during grid disturbance" |

### 2.2 Lens

> **"What [supply / dispatch / safeguard] does this [cold-storage operator] need [to keep refrigeration loads powered at the lowest landed €/kWh while never breaking the temperature chain]?"**

Lens source: VN node `job_family` of L6H-POWER ("Supply reliable electrical energy; manage cost and resilience").

### 2.3 Core Job + Product Job

**Core Job (P2, solution-independent):**
> *"Keep our cold-store running at the lowest landed energy cost without ever letting the freezers warm above SLA."*

**Product Job (anchored at L5 BESS, references parent):**
> *"Help the cold-store's Energy Supply & Electrical Utilities to deliver continuous, demand-charge-optimized electrical energy to refrigeration and operational loads while preserving HACCP-grade temperature continuity through grid sags, brownouts, and tariff peaks."*

Abstraction test: 5 alternatives from different categories exist → ✅ Core Job (not scenario).

### 2.4 Alternatives (5)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC (proxy) | Inherent trade-offs | Existential? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Multi-MWh Li-ion BESS EPC** (Mein Solar's slot) | 26111711 | CAPEX €250–400/kWh, fire/thermal-runaway risk, 6–12 mo cert lead, MV grid required | — |
| A2 | Diesel / gas standby genset only | 26101800 | Diesel cost & CO₂, 60–120 s start delay (loses sub-cycle ride-through), no peak-shave revenue, BImSchV emission limits | — |
| A3 | DSO grid-capacity upgrade (higher contracted kW) | 83101800 | 1–3 yr DSO lead time, capacity may be denied in saturated rural feeders, no resilience benefit, recurring Netzentgelte burden | — |
| A4 | Behind-the-meter PV + minimal storage | 26111605 / 41112113 | PV alone cannot cover 24/7 reefer baseload, intermittent, weather-dependent, undersized BESS gives no real peak-shave headroom | — |
| A5 | Do nothing — pay demand charges & accept outage risk | — | Zero capex, zero project burden — but 60–440 €/kW/yr Leistungspreis recurring + uninsured spoilage exposure | **YES** (eliminates BESS purchase entirely) |

**Merge test:** distinct alternative sets per scenario → no merge.
**Cross-cat test:** chemistry (A1), combustion (A2), grid contract (A3), generation (A4), accept-loss (A5) → ✅ Core Job (not scenario).

### 2.5 Scenarios (output-type activation)

| Scenario | Activates output type | Activates L6H-POWER L5 |
|---|---|---|
| Sc1 — Daily peak-shave during 17:00–20:00 demand window | Chilled, Frozen, Deep-freeze | BESS, EMS, MV |
| Sc2 — Grid sag / brownout (sub-cycle to 30 min) | All — protect temp chain | BESS, UPS, MV |
| Sc3 — Sustained grid outage > 30 min | All — temp-chain survival | BESS + Genset interlock |
| Sc4 — Blast-freeze cycle compressor inrush | Deep-freeze IQF | BESS, PCS |
| Sc5 — Intraday EPEX arbitrage (negative-price hours) | Charge during PV / wind surplus | BESS + EMS + market interface |
| Sc6 — Reefer-truck HPC charging window | Outbound dispatch | HPC + BESS buffer |
| Sc7 — FCR / aFRR ancillary participation | Surplus capacity monetisation | BESS + EMS + aggregator |

Sparsity check: scenarios activate distinct asset combinations → ✅ Core Job is not too coarse.

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Needs Derivation
sections.content:
### Stakeholder roles (per Burleson + Ulwick)

| Role | Person in cold-store context | Active levels |
|---|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | Stored goods (proxy: food-safety officer & end customer) | P2, P5 |
| Job Executor | Facility / Energy Manager — operates BESS dispatch | P1, P2, P3, P4, P5 |
| Job Overseer | COO / Plant Director — accountable for SLA and €/pallet | P2 own, P4 |
| Lifecycle Support | Specifier (consulting engineer), Commissioner (TÜV), Maintainer (O&M) | P1 own stage |
| Purchase Executor | CFO / Asset Owner — signs the EPC contract | P1-Acquisition, P4 |
| Purchase Influencer | Insurance carrier (Allianz GVT), IFS/BRC auditor, KfW lender | P2 own, P4 |

### Job Map relevance (Ulwick 8 steps, Product Job context)

| # | Step | Relevant to BESS EPC | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | ✅ | Sizing kW/kWh against load profile, SLA, revenue stack |
| 2 | LOCATE | ✅ | Site, MV feeder, financing, certification body |
| 3 | PREPARE | ✅ | Foundation, MV cabinet, fire clearance, Baugenehmigung |
| 4 | CONFIRM | ✅ | VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8, fire & insurance sign-off |
| 5 | EXECUTE | ✅ | Daily dispatch, ride-through, peak-shave, arbitrage |
| 6 | MONITOR | ✅ | SoH, market signals, refrigeration-load interlock |
| 7 | MODIFY | ⚠️ partial | Re-tune EMS as tariff or load shifts — owned by O&M |
| 8 | CONCLUDE | ⚠️ partial | EoL replacement, second-life — 10+ years out |

**Relevant steps: 6 (DEFINE…MONITOR).** Yield target: `max(15, min(30, 6×4)) = 24 needs`. Will produce **24 ErrorStatements**.

---

### 3.1 Burleson Pyramid (per role, per alternative — summary)

P1 Product Jobs differ sharply by alternative — illustrative for Energy Manager (Executor):

| Stage | A1 BESS | A2 Genset | A3 Grid upgrade | A4 PV+small | A5 Do nothing |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Acquire | EPC contract, finance, permit | OEM order, fuel supply | DSO application, EU tender | EPC, roof survey | — |
| Prepare | Foundation, MV cabinet, fire | Fuel tank, BImSchV permit | Wait 1–3 yr | Roof works, inverter | — |
| Use | Dispatch SoC, refrig interlock | Monthly test-runs | None | PV monitoring | Pay demand charge |
| Maintain | Annual cert, BMS, HVAC | Oil, fuel, emissions | None | Module wash, inverter | — |
| Dispose | Cell recycling (EU Battery Reg) | Diesel-tank decom | None | Module recycling | — |

A5 has zero P1 burden — **always the strongest competitor**.

### 3.2 + 3.3 — 24 Error Statements (P1 + P2 + P3-P5 mix)

All needs are **solution-agnostic** ("storage solution", "energy supply solution") per V6 rule. `pr` = product_related (true = caused by the BESS technology class itself).

| # | Job step | Need (Minimize / Increase) | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | pr | Conf |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N1 | DEFINE | Increase the ability to size storage capacity to within ±10 % of the demand-charge-shave that will actually clear in year-1 operation | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | 0.85 |
| N2 | DEFINE | Minimize the likelihood of underestimating refrigeration peak-load duration by >15 minutes during the daily 17:00-20:00 Leistungspreis window | 9 | 5 | 13 | cost | false | 0.83 |
| N3 | DEFINE | Increase the ability to forecast HACCP-relevant load survival time at <30 % SoC with ≥95 % accuracy | 8 | 3 | 13 | safety | true | 0.80 |
| N4 | DEFINE | Minimize the likelihood of mis-stacking incompatible revenue streams (peak-shave vs FCR vs §19 StromNEV) costing >5 % of business case | 8 | 3 | 13 | cost | false | 0.82 |
| N5 | LOCATE | Minimize the likelihood that DSO MV-feeder spare-capacity assessment reverses after 6+ months of project work | 10 | 4 | 16 | reliability | false | 0.90 |
| N6 | LOCATE | Increase the ability to secure FGW-accredited certification slots without >4 weeks of schedule slip | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | false | 0.78 |
| N7 | LOCATE | Minimize the likelihood that fire-clearance distance to refrigeration plant or IFS-bonded storage exceeds available footprint | 9 | 4 | 14 | safety | true | 0.84 |
| N8 | LOCATE | Increase the ability to obtain insurance terms that recognize Li-ion containment design without >25 % premium uplift | 7 | 3 | 11 | cost | true | 0.75 |
| N9 | PREPARE | Minimize the likelihood of foundation, MV-cabinet, and refrigeration-plant works colliding on the construction critical path | 8 | 4 | 12 | time | false | 0.80 |
| N10 | PREPARE | Increase the ability to interlock storage-system control with NH₃/CO₂ refrigeration safety logic without retrofitting refrigeration PLC | 8 | 3 | 13 | safety | false | 0.82 |
| N11 | PREPARE | Minimize the likelihood of operator training gaps causing HACCP-procedure non-compliance during commissioning | 7 | 5 | 9 | skill | false | 0.72 |
| N12 | CONFIRM | Minimize the likelihood that fire-protection commissioning fails on first inspection, delaying COD by >30 days | 9 | 4 | 14 | safety | true | 0.85 |
| N13 | CONFIRM | Increase the ability to demonstrate sub-100 ms ride-through to NH₃ refrigeration controllers with witnessed test evidence | 9 | 3 | 15 | safety | false | 0.83 |
| N14 | CONFIRM | Minimize the likelihood of insurance auditor flagging missing IEC 62443 cybersecurity controls before COD | 6 | 4 | 8 | safety | true | 0.70 |
| N15 | EXECUTE | Increase the ability to absorb compressor-start inrush of >3× nominal without tripping MV protection | 9 | 4 | 14 | reliability | false | 0.86 |
| N16 | EXECUTE | Minimize the likelihood of dispatch error letting any chamber exceed +2 °C above SLA setpoint during grid disturbance | 10 | 3 | 17 | safety | false | 0.92 |
| N17 | EXECUTE | Increase the ability to capture EPEX intraday arbitrage on >300 days/yr without compromising HACCP reserve SoC | 8 | 3 | 13 | cost | false | 0.80 |
| N18 | EXECUTE | Minimize the likelihood of cell-cycle degradation exceeding 2 %/yr from arbitrage cycling above C/2 | 7 | 5 | 9 | reliability | true | 0.78 |
| N19 | MONITOR | Increase the ability to detect early thermal-runaway precursors with ≥30 minute lead time before suppression activation | 10 | 3 | 17 | safety | true | 0.88 |
| N20 | MONITOR | Minimize the likelihood of HACCP audit finding gaps in temperature-chain evidence during BESS-mediated grid events | 9 | 4 | 14 | safety | false | 0.83 |
| N21 | MONITOR | Increase the ability to expose live SoH and reserved-resilience-SoC to the WMS / SCADA layer in real time | 7 | 3 | 11 | reliability | true | 0.74 |
| N22 | MONITOR | Minimize the likelihood of EMS over-discharging below the contractually-reserved spoilage-protection envelope | 9 | 3 | 15 | safety | true | 0.85 |
| N23 | EXECUTE (P3 Role) | Increase the ability of the Energy Manager to demonstrate measurable €/MWh savings to the COO every quarter | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | false | 0.70 |
| N24 | CONFIRM (P5 Emotion) | Minimize the likelihood of nighttime spoilage anxiety for the duty manager — visible reserved-resilience SoC at all times `[hypothesis]` | 6 | 2 | 10 | reliability | true | 0.55 |

### 3.4 ODI Roll-up

| Bucket | Threshold | Count | Needs |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Underserved** (imp >7 AND sat <5) | strongest opportunity | **15** | N1, N2, N3, N4, N5, N7, N9, N10, N12, N13, N15, N16, N17, N19, N20, N22 (16 by strict count incl. N9 sat=4) |
| Overserved (imp <3 AND sat >7) | resource elsewhere | 0 | — |
| Mid-tier | balance | 8 | N6, N8, N11, N14, N18, N21, N23, N24 |

**Top 5 by opportunity score:**
1. **N16** — Dispatch error letting chamber exceed +2 °C SLA (opp **17**, safety, market-inherent)
2. **N19** — Thermal-runaway early detection (opp **17**, safety, tech-specific)
3. **N5** — DSO capacity reversal mid-project (opp **16**, reliability, market-inherent)
4. **N13** — Sub-100 ms ride-through to NH₃ controllers (opp **15**, safety, market-inherent)
5. **N22** — EMS over-discharge below resilience envelope (opp **15**, safety, tech-specific)

### Yield-target check

| Metric | Target | Actual | Pass |
|---|---|---|---|
| Statements per market | 15-30 | 24 | ✅ |
| Needs per relevant step | ≥2 | min 3 (DEFINE 4, LOCATE 4, PREPARE 3, CONFIRM 4, EXECUTE 5, MONITOR 4) | ✅ |
| product_related=false ratio | 60-70 % | 14/24 = 58 % | ⚠️ on boundary — acceptable for tech-heavy cold-store BESS |
| product_related=true ratio | 30-40 % | 10/24 = 42 % | ⚠️ slight over — driven by Li-ion-inherent fire/cycle/EMS risks |
| Minimize vs Increase | ~60/40 | 13 Min / 11 Inc = 54/46 | ✅ |
| Job-map steps | 8 exact | 8 (6 relevant) | ✅ |

---

sections.title: QA — V1-V8 Validation
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| V1 | Format compliance (Minimize/Increase + 1 KPI) | ✅ |
| V2 | product_related correctly classified | ✅ (technology-caused needs all reference Li-ion/PCS/cells) |
| V3 | No unrealistic imp/sat patterns | ✅ |
| V4 | Impact category matches KPI | ✅ |
| V5 | Coverage ≥2/step, mix close to 60/40 | ✅ |
| V6 | Solution-agnostic — no vendor/product names | ✅ |
| V7 | Anchor level matches abstraction (system → L5) | ✅ |
| V8 | Product job references parent (Energy Supply & Electrical Utilities) | ✅ |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [Energy Efficiency in Refrigerated Warehouses (Green Design Consulting)](https://www.greendesignconsulting.com/single-post/energy-efficiency-in-refrigerated-warehouses) — refrigeration = 60-80 % of cold-store electricity
- [Cold Storage Energy Management (Inertia Resources)](https://www.inertiaresourcesinc.com/cold-storage-energy.php) — energy 60-70 % of facility opex
- [Peak Shaving — Lastspitzenkappung (Next Kraftwerke)](https://www.next-kraftwerke.de/wissen/peak-shaving) — DE Leistungspreis 60-440 €/kW/yr (ene't Jan-2024); ~180 €/kW typical
- [Krannich Solar — Peakshaving mit Gewerbespeicher](https://krannich-solar.com/de-de/blog/peakshaving/) — payback 3-7 yr; viable from 100k kWh/yr + 100 kW peaks
- [Cold Storage and the Cost of a Single Degree (Megawattage)](https://www.megawattage.com/blog/cold-storage-and-the-cost-of-a-single-degree/) — single extended outage can erase years of profit
- [USDA / FoodSafety.gov — Food Safety During Power Outage](https://www.foodsafety.gov/food-safety-charts/food-safety-during-power-outage) — discard perishables after 4 h without power
- [VDE-AR-N 4110 — MV Grid Connection Rules (VDE)](https://www.vde.com/en/fnn/topics/technical-connection-rules/tcr-for-medium-voltage)
- [2025 Germany Energy Storage Market Guide (PVB)](https://www.pvb.com/blog/2025-germany-energy-storage-market-guide-policies-bess-selection-industry-trends-complete-citations/) — DE C&I BESS regulatory + revenue stack
- [Moment of truth — 2026 regulatory agenda for large battery storage in Germany (Energy-Storage.News)](https://www.energy-storage.news/moment-of-truth-the-2026-regulatory-agenda-for-large-battery-storage-in-germany/)

rows.Value: Preserve the physical integrity, temperature chain, and legal compliance of perishable goods from inbound receipt through outbound dispatch, with auditable traceability and regulatory conformance
Chilled storage (+2 to +8 °C), Frozen storage (-18 to -25 °C), Deep-freeze / blast (-25 to -40 °C), Pharma-GDP storage (+2 to +8 °C, validated), Cross-dock & VAS
L7 ecosystem — service NAICS, no Product BOM

rows.Defining circumstance: Nordfrost, Frigoscandia, Constellation; multi-customer SLA, mixed temp zones, 24/7 dispatch
Nestlé, Unilever frozen, dairies; single-product, attached to plant, batch-driven
DHL Pharma, World Courier; +2 to +8 °C, full audit trail, IQ/OQ/PQ on every system
REWE Frischlogistik, Edeka regional DC, Picnic; high-throughput pick, peak loads at dawn
Iglo, Frosta tolling lines; massive intermittent compressor load during freeze cycles

rows.Value: Job context = "supply reliable, cost-optimized electrical energy to all cold-store loads (refrigeration, MHE, IT/OT, lighting, EV) and protect HACCP temperature chain during grid disturbance"

rows.Inherent trade-offs: CAPEX €250–400/kWh, fire/thermal-runaway risk, 6–12 mo cert lead, MV grid required
Diesel cost & CO₂, 60–120 s start delay (loses sub-cycle ride-through), no peak-shave revenue, BImSchV emission limits
1–3 yr DSO lead time, capacity may be denied in saturated rural feeders, no resilience benefit, recurring Netzentgelte burden
PV alone cannot cover 24/7 reefer baseload, intermittent, weather-dependent, undersized BESS gives no real peak-shave headroom

rows.Alternative: Do nothing — pay demand charges & accept outage risk
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Zero capex, zero project burden — but 60–440 €/kW/yr Leistungspreis recurring + uninsured spoilage exposure

rows.Scenario: Sc1 — Daily peak-shave during 17:00–20:00 demand window
Sc2 — Grid sag / brownout (sub-cycle to 30 min)

rows.Person in cold-store context: Stored goods (proxy: food-safety officer & end customer)
Facility / Energy Manager — operates BESS dispatch
COO / Plant Director — accountable for SLA and €/pallet
CFO / Asset Owner — signs the EPC contract
Insurance carrier (Allianz GVT), IFS/BRC auditor, KfW lender

rows.Why: Sizing kW/kWh against load profile, SLA, revenue stack
Site, MV feeder, financing, certification body
Foundation, MV cabinet, fire clearance, Baugenehmigung
VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8, fire & insurance sign-off
Re-tune EMS as tariff or load shifts — owned by O&M

rows.Need (Minimize / Increase): Increase the ability to size storage capacity to within ±10 % of the demand-charge-shave that will actually clear in year-1 operation
Minimize the likelihood of underestimating refrigeration peak-load duration by >15 minutes during the daily 17:00-20:00 Leistungspreis window
Increase the ability to forecast HACCP-relevant load survival time at <30 % SoC with ≥95 % accuracy
Minimize the likelihood of mis-stacking incompatible revenue streams (peak-shave vs FCR vs §19 StromNEV) costing >5 % of business case
Minimize the likelihood that DSO MV-feeder spare-capacity assessment reverses after 6+ months of project work
Increase the ability to secure FGW-accredited certification slots without >4 weeks of schedule slip
Minimize the likelihood that fire-clearance distance to refrigeration plant or IFS-bonded storage exceeds available footprint
Increase the ability to obtain insurance terms that recognize Li-ion containment design without >25 % premium uplift
Minimize the likelihood of foundation, MV-cabinet, and refrigeration-plant works colliding on the construction critical path
Increase the ability to interlock storage-system control with NH₃/CO₂ refrigeration safety logic without retrofitting refrigeration PLC
Minimize the likelihood of operator training gaps causing HACCP-procedure non-compliance during commissioning
Minimize the likelihood that fire-protection commissioning fails on first inspection, delaying COD by >30 days
Increase the ability to demonstrate sub-100 ms ride-through to NH₃ refrigeration controllers with witnessed test evidence
Minimize the likelihood of insurance auditor flagging missing IEC 62443 cybersecurity controls before COD
Increase the ability to absorb compressor-start inrush of >3× nominal without tripping MV protection
Minimize the likelihood of dispatch error letting any chamber exceed +2 °C above SLA setpoint during grid disturbance
Increase the ability to capture EPEX intraday arbitrage on >300 days/yr without compromising HACCP reserve SoC
Minimize the likelihood of cell-cycle degradation exceeding 2 %/yr from arbitrage cycling above C/2
Increase the ability to detect early thermal-runaway precursors with ≥30 minute lead time before suppression activation
Minimize the likelihood of HACCP audit finding gaps in temperature-chain evidence during BESS-mediated grid events
Increase the ability to expose live SoH and reserved-resilience-SoC to the WMS / SCADA layer in real time
Minimize the likelihood of EMS over-discharging below the contractually-reserved spoilage-protection envelope
Increase the ability of the Energy Manager to demonstrate measurable €/MWh savings to the COO every quarter
Minimize the likelihood of nighttime spoilage anxiety for the duty manager — visible reserved-resilience SoC at all times `[hypothesis]`

rows.Needs: N1, N2, N3, N4, N5, N7, N9, N10, N12, N13, N15, N16, N17, N19, N20, N22 (16 by strict count incl. N9 sat=4)

rows.Actual: min 3 (DEFINE 4, LOCATE 4, PREPARE 3, CONFIRM 4, EXECUTE 5, MONITOR 4)

rows.Pass: ⚠️ on boundary — acceptable for tech-heavy cold-store BESS
⚠️ slight over — driven by Li-ion-inherent fire/cycle/EMS risks

rows.Check: Product job references parent (Energy Supply & Electrical Utilities)

entities.cfj_statement: Preserve the physical integrity, temperature chain, and legal compliance of perishable goods from inbound receipt through outbound dispatch, with auditable traceability and regulatory conformance
entities.product_job_statement: Help the cold-store's Energy Supply & Electrical Utilities to deliver continuous, demand-charge-optimized electrical energy to refrigeration and operational loads while preserving HACCP-grade temperature continuity through grid sags, brownouts, and tariff peaks

segments.name: Multi-tenant 3PL cold chain
segments.circumstance: multi-customer SLA, mixed temp zones, 24/7 dispatch

segments.name: Quick-commerce / grocery DC
segments.circumstance: high-throughput pick, peak loads at dawn

segments.name: Blast-freeze / IQF processor
segments.circumstance: intermittent compressor load during freeze cycles

entities.lens: What supply / dispatch / safeguard does this cold-storage operator need to keep refrigeration loads powered at the lowest landed €/kWh while never breaking the temperature chain?
entities.core_job: Keep our cold-store running at the lowest landed energy cost without ever letting the freezers warm above SLA.

alternatives.name: DSO grid-capacity upgrade
trade_offs: may be denied in saturated feeders

alternatives.name: Do nothing — pay demand charges & accept outage risk

stakeholder_roles.person: Stored goods (proxy: food-safety officer, end customer)
Insurance carrier, IFS/BRC auditor, KfW lender

job_map.relevance_rationale: Sizing kW/kWh against load profile, SLA, revenue stack
Site, MV feeder, financing, certification body
Foundation, MV cabinet, fire clearance, Baugenehmigung
VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8, fire & insurance sign-off
Re-tune EMS owned by O&M provider, partial scope
EoL replacement / second-life is 10+ yr out, partial scope

incumbent_anchors.rationale: Dominant incumbent for outage protection; no peak-shave or arbitrage revenue
Generation-only, intermittent, cannot cover 24/7 reefer baseload

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to size storage capacity to within ±10 % of the demand-charge-shave that will actually clear in year-1 operation
error_statements.rationale: Cold-store load profiles are flat-baseline + spike-driven; sizing tools designed for PV self-consumption mis-predict shave value

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of underestimating refrigeration peak-load duration by >15 minutes during the daily 17:00-20:00 Leistungspreis window
error_statements.rationale: Compressor cycling lengthens true peak duration; ene't 60-440 €/kW Leistungspreis penalises any 15-min interval breach

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to forecast HACCP-relevant load survival time at <30 % SoC with ≥95 % accuracy
error_statements.rationale: Li-ion reserved-SoC envelope vs refrigeration thermal mass = facility-specific calculation rarely done at design stage

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of mis-stacking incompatible revenue streams (peak-shave vs FCR vs §19 StromNEV) costing >5 % of business case
error_statements.rationale: §19 StromNEV atypical-grid-usage requires flat 7000h/yr profile — incompatible with FCR cycling

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that DSO MV-feeder spare-capacity assessment reverses after 6+ months of project work
error_statements.rationale: Rural DE feeders saturated by PV/wind; capacity letters non-binding until grid contract signed

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to secure FGW-accredited certification slots without >4 weeks of schedule slip
error_statements.rationale: Limited TÜV/FGW capacity vs wave of BESS projects 2025-2027

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that fire-clearance distance to refrigeration plant or IFS-bonded storage exceeds available footprint
error_statements.rationale: Li-ion fire clearances clash with NH3 plant rooms and IFS food-grade buffer zones

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to obtain insurance terms that recognize Li-ion containment design without >25 % premium uplift
error_statements.rationale: Specialty fire underwriters lack actuarial data on co-located cold-store BESS

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of foundation, MV-cabinet, and refrigeration-plant works colliding on the construction critical path
error_statements.rationale: Live cold-store cannot stop refrigeration during BESS works → night-shift/weekend coordination

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to interlock storage-system control with NH₃/CO₂ refrigeration safety logic without retrofitting refrigeration PLC
error_statements.rationale: Refrigeration PLCs are EN 378 safety-rated, change-controlled; OEM resists 3rd-party signal injection

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of operator training gaps causing HACCP-procedure non-compliance during commissioning
error_statements.rationale: Cold-store operators have no Li-ion training tradition; HACCP requires documented competence

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that fire-protection commissioning fails on first inspection, delaying COD by >30 days
error_statements.rationale: VdS Li-ion guidance still maturing; local Feuerwehr have inconsistent acceptance criteria

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to demonstrate sub-100 ms ride-through to NH₃ refrigeration controllers with witnessed test evidence
error_statements.rationale: NH3 plant trips on 200 ms voltage dip → leak risk; storage solution must prove sub-cycle support

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of insurance auditor flagging missing IEC 62443 cybersecurity controls before COD
error_statements.rationale: Cyber-physical control over refrigeration via storage EMS = new attack surface for insurer

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to absorb compressor-start inrush of >3× nominal without tripping MV protection
error_statements.rationale: Refrigeration compressors draw 3-7× FLA at start; MV protection trip = SLA breach risk

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of dispatch error letting any chamber exceed +2 °C above SLA setpoint during grid disturbance
error_statements.rationale: +2 °C breach in pharma/frozen = HACCP non-conformance + product write-off; existential risk

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to capture EPEX intraday arbitrage on >300 days/yr without compromising HACCP reserve SoC
error_statements.rationale: Generic EMS optimises €/MWh first; HACCP reserve as hard constraint is rare

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of cell-cycle degradation exceeding 2 %/yr from arbitrage cycling above C/2
error_statements.rationale: Aggressive arbitrage cycles erode warranty headroom; LFP at C/2 has documented 2 %/yr threshold

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to detect early thermal-runaway precursors with ≥30 minute lead time before suppression activation
error_statements.rationale: Cold-store next to flammable insulation/refrigerant; 30 min lead enables Feuerwehr arrival

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of HACCP audit finding gaps in temperature-chain evidence during BESS-mediated grid events
error_statements.rationale: Auditors require time-aligned voltage + temperature trace; standard EMS lacks T-chain export

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to expose live SoH and reserved-resilience-SoC to the WMS / SCADA layer in real time
error_statements.rationale: WMS/SCADA integration is afterthought in most BESS deployments

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of EMS over-discharging below the contractually-reserved spoilage-protection envelope
error_statements.rationale: Hard reserved-SoC floor as contractual SLA is non-standard in current EMS products

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability of the Energy Manager to demonstrate measurable €/MWh savings to the COO every quarter (P3 Role)
error_statements.rationale: P3 role-identity need: Energy Manager becomes the cost-saver; needs auditable evidence

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of nighttime spoilage anxiety for the duty manager — visible reserved-resilience SoC at all times (P5 Emotion) [hypothesis]
error_statements.rationale: P5 emotional bridge: duty manager needs visible 'safety reserve' indicator to sleep at night

top_5_by_opportunity.summary: Sub-100 ms ride-through to NH₃ controllers
